title: Tail behavior of random variables
summary: Concentration and tail-bound theory for sub-Gaussian and sub-exponential random variables, including Hoeffding-type MGF bounds and exponential tail inequalities.

theorems.name: Sub-Gaussian one-sided tail bound
theorems.blurb: A mean-zero sigma^2-sub-Gaussian random variable has its right tail bounded by a Gaussian exponential decay.

theorems.name: Sub-Gaussian two-sided tail bound
theorems.blurb: A mean-zero sub-Gaussian random variable concentrates around zero with a two-sided exponential tail.

theorems.name: Hoeffding's lemma (bounded implies sub-Gaussian)
theorems.blurb: A bounded mean-zero random variable is sub-Gaussian with proxy variance (b-a)^2/4, giving the classical Hoeffding MGF bound.

theorems.name: Sub-exponential Bernstein tail bound
theorems.blurb: A mean-zero sub-exponential random variable obeys a Bernstein-type tail that is Gaussian for small t and exponential for large t.

theorems.name: Sub-exponential MGF bound for squared sub-Gaussian
theorems.blurb: The square of a sub-Gaussian random variable is sub-exponential, with an explicit moment generating function bound.
